creator: yasu@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 232672
Patch file

Downgrade to 21.0.0.

According to the release information of 22.0.0, minimum version requirement of py-cryptography is bumped to 35.0.

https://pypi.org/project/pyOpenSSL/#id1

On the other hand current version of security/py-cryptography is 3.3.2. Since it doesn't meet the requirement, runtime error happens when this port is used by consumer. So downgrade to 21.0.0 whose minimum version requirement of py-cryptography is 3.3.

While I'm here,

* Remove remnant of supporting already EoL'ed FreeBSD 11.x.
* Pet portclippy.
* Tidy up Makefile with portfmt.

Fixes:  ad1bc60d6ae8 (security/py-openssl: update to 22.0.0)
